subject: Computer Networking
title: SQL Injection
description: A micro-lesson that makes SQL Injection usable.

hook.text: Type a name into a form. But instead of a name, type SQL code. If the server runs it, you own the database.

buildup.text: SQL injection happens when user input is inserted into a query without sanitization. The input becomes code.

discovery.text: A classic attack: ' OR 1=1 -- . This makes the WHERE clause always true, returning all rows.

twist.text: Parameterized queries separate code from data. The input is always treated as a value, never as SQL.

climax.text: SQL injection remains in OWASP's Top 10 after 20 years. Old, well-known, and still exploited.

punchline.text: Never trust user input. Parameterize every query. Always.

quiz.question: What is the best defense against SQL injection?
options: Using parameterized queries that separate data from code
Limiting the length of user input fields
Encrypting the database connection with TLS
